display.purpose: App title and rare empty-state headings.

mono.purpose: Paths, IDs, shells, tokens, and timestamps.

shadows.name: hairline-rest
shadows.purpose: Tiny lift for top bar or selected controls.

motion.name: control-fast
motion.purpose: Buttons, tabs, chips, and row hover.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary local action, such as refresh or focus pane.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(0.66 0.12 150); color: oklch(0.24 0.02 95); border: 1px solid oklch(0.58 0.10 150); border-radius: 10px; padding: 10px 14px; font: 600 12px/1.4 IBM Plex Sans, Manrope, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background-color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), border-color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.70 0.12 150); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.72 0.12 150 / 0.35); }

components.name: Local Status Chip
components.description: Small trust signal for local monitoring state.
components.css: .ds-chip-local { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: oklch(0.89 0.05 150); color: oklch(0.24 0.02 95); border: 1px solid oklch(0.78 0.06 150); border-radius: 999px; padding: 4px 10px; font: 600 12px/1.4 IBM Plex Sans, Manrope, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-chip-local::before { content: ''; width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 999px; background: oklch(0.66 0.12 150); }

components.name: Summary Card
components.description: Compact usage summary with one dominant reading.
components.css: .ds-summary-card { background: oklch(0.95 0.012 95); color: oklch(0.24 0.02 95); border: 1px solid oklch(0.82 0.014 95); border-radius: 16px; padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch(0.24 0.02 95 / 0.06); } .ds-summary-label { margin: 0 0 8px; color: oklch(0.44 0.02 95); font: 600 12px/1.4 IBM Plex Sans, Manrope, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-summary-value { margin: 0; font: 600 28px/1.2 Fraunces, Source Serif 4, Georgia, serif; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

narrative.overview: AI Shepherd should look like a calm local instrument beside the user's editor and terminal. It is warm, disciplined, and easy to scan, with enough technical detail to earn trust and enough softness to avoid management-console coldness.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained color strategy: tinted neutrals plus one moss accent under 10 percent of a screen.
Local-first posture visible through status, copy, and hierarchy.

rules.name: The Ten Percent Moss Rule
rules.body: Moss is a signal, not decoration. If every card or row uses it, the monitor stops being glanceable.

rules.name: The No Neon Rule
rules.body: Prohibited: neon-on-black, glowing outlines, purple SaaS gradients, and synthetic terminal theatrics.

rules.name: The Flat At Rest Rule
rules.body: Lists, cards, and panels are calm until interaction. Hover may shift color or shadow, but never layout.

dos: Do use daylight neutrals and moss accent to make local monitoring feel calm and trustworthy.
Do keep the first read glanceable: active state, last refresh, pending prompts, and current usage.
Do show technical detail where it helps: model, source file, terminal pane, cwd, shell, tokens, and timestamps.
donts: Don't use crypto-terminal styling: neon-on-black, hacker theatrics, aggressive contrast, or synthetic glow.
Don't use enterprise admin dashboards: cold CRUD tables, generic panels, dense chrome, or management-console language.
Don't use AI SaaS clichés: purple gradients, glass cards, hype copy, generic hero metrics, or unlock insights language.
